mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is rising on a 3-month annualized basis.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate falls below a critical threshold, it indicates aggressive monetary easing by the central bank. This action aims to stimulate the economy by lowering borrowing costs, which typically supports equity valuations by compressing discount rates and boosting liquidity, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS has fallen below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and significant decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and investment. This dual weakness signals a severe economic downturn, negatively impacting corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns as recessionary fears intensify.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 30% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST are declining significantly as per the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a further decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals deepening recessionary pressures and reduced manufacturing activity. This translates to lower corporate earnings expectations and increased investor pessimism, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 35% of triggered instances during identified contractionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Significant job losses, as indicated by a sharp decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflect rapidly weakening labor market conditions and eroding consumer confidence. This directly impacts future corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to immediate negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 50% of periods following a triggered significant drop in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low or falling inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides the central bank with flexibility to maintain accommodative monetary policy. This environment supports equity valuations by keeping discount rates low and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL is stable or declining on a 3-month annualized basis.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) signals an official recession, leading to widespread corporate profit declines and heightened investor pessimism. This fundamental economic weakness drives S&P 500 returns lower over the medium term as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of periods following a confirmed GDP contraction.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is being aggressively cut (signaling monetary easing) while unemployment (UNRATE) is rising rapidly, it indicates a severe economic crisis where monetary policy is struggling to offset fundamental weakness. This combination suggests deep systemic issues, leading to continued negative equity returns despite easing efforts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 30% of triggered instances where both aggressive FEDFUNDS cuts and rising UNRATE are observed.
